metadata.source: Auto-generated from JMdict verb and adjective entries
metadata.conjugation_rules_reference: Conjugation rules encoded directly in build/transform/conjugations.py. They follow the standard ichidan/godan conjugation rules taught in any introductory Japanese grammar reference. Any incorrectness is a bug in the rule implementation, not in the data.
metadata.attribution: Conjugation tables auto-generated from JMdict verb and adjective entries (EDRDG License, CC-BY-SA 4.0). Rules are formally defined and deterministic; no native-speaker review is required for the conjugation logic itself.

field_notes.dictionary_form: The kanji writing of the lemma, or kana if kana-only.
field_notes.reading: The kana reading used as the conjugation stem.
field_notes.class: JMdict POS tag indicating the word class used for conjugation.
field_notes.forms: Map from form name to conjugated kana form. Specific forms available depend on the word class.
field_notes.v1: Ichidan verbs: 食べる, 見る, 起きる, etc.
field_notes.v5u-v5m: Godan verbs grouped by the kana ending of the dictionary form.
field_notes.adj-i: い-adjectives: 高い, 新しい, 小さい, etc.
field_notes.adj-na: な-adjectives: 静か, 元気, etc. (conjugate via the copula).
field_notes.potential_ichidan_note: Modern colloquial Japanese often drops the ら in ichidan potential forms (ら抜き言葉), e.g., 食べられる → 食べれる. We generate the traditional form; the ら-less form is grammatically nonstandard but widely used.
